cycle: scheduled-live-ensemble (2 cycles this date; NSHA re-researched in cycle 2 after Shah formally declared)
arbiter: opus-4.8 (Fable quota deferred; desktop default model)
ensemble: 2x opus (inside/outside personas) + 2x codex gpt-5.5 xhigh, all blind (no market prices); round-2 focused re-estimate after verification per PROTOCOL 3.5 / VALUES.md #3.

items.title: Fed hikes 25bps at the July 29, 2026 FOMC meeting
items.rationale: [CYCLE 1] CORE: consensus ~0.24 (opus 0.225 / codex 0.26; family divergence 0.035, tight) vs Kalshi H25 ask 0.15 -> ~+9pt YES edge. RISK: settlement needs EXACTLY a 25bp hike on Jul 29; base case is still 'wait-and-see' hold, and Chair Warsh (Jul 1) did NOT commit to July, so the first hike could slip to Sept. VERIFICATION: May CPI 4.2% YoY / May PCE >4% (3yr high, Iran-war energy shock); June dots show 9/18 FOMC now see >=1 hike by year-end; cross-venue July-hike odds 21-30% -> Kalshi's 14.5% is an outlier LOW. Edge 0.095 < 0.10 -> normal tier. Executed cycle 1 (now hold 9 @ 15c).

items.title: Nirav Shah is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Maine as of Jul 31, 2026
items.rationale: [CYCLE 2 re-research; Shah declared 07-09 AM] CORE: Platner dropped out 07-08 (assault allegation); replacement picked by a 600-delegate convention (500 county-proportional + 100 state-committee) by the 07-27 statutory deadline. Round-1 blind ensemble (0.21-0.34) ran WELL above market 0.12 because it lacked the delegate/endorsement map. VERIFICATION (mandatory) closed the gap: Troy Jackson is the consolidated frontrunner (only FEC filer, ~52% external), Jackson + Sec. of State Shenna Bellows CROSS-ENDORSED and campaign together, and Our Revolution (progressive PAC) endorsed Jackson -- establishment AND labor/progressive blocs both behind one man. RISK: delegate-count surprise or Jackson stumble; Shah retains statewide COVID-era name ID. DIVERGENCE: round-1 co-leader premise vs verified Jackson-consolidated insider convention. Both families' round-2 finals collapsed to 0.11 (Codex independently re-verified AP/Guardian). Consensus 0.11 ~= market 0.12 -> NO EDGE, no trade. We already hold TJAC NO in this same event.

items.title: Kash Patel leaves as FBI Director before Aug 1, 2026
items.rationale: [CYCLE 2] CORE: resolves YES only if Patel actually VACATES the role before Aug 1 (~3-week window); Schumer/Durbin resignation demands have no procedural power and Trump (sole decider) has signaled no removal. The Atlantic reporting (drinking/absenteeism) + FBI purges create genuine tail risk but no departure event. RISK: sudden Trump decision if he deems Patel a liability. DIVERGENCE: Opus weighted the controversy tail higher (0.08-0.10) than Codex (0.04); family divergence 0.05, within tolerance -> not a crux, no round 2. Consensus ~0.065 vs market ~0.05; edge below the 0.035-after-fees threshold and yes bid/ask (0.023/0.052) is wide -> no tradable edge, no trade.

items.title: Google (Gemini) is the #1 LMArena Overall model on July 31, 2026
items.rationale: [CYCLE 1] VERIFICATION VOIDED THE BLIND (PROTOCOL 3.5): blind used a stale June snapshot (Gemini ~#3). Live Arena Overall board (Jul 1) top 5 all Anthropic; Google's best is #7 (~23 ELO back). Gemini 3.5 Pro delayed to ~Jul 17 (public, priced). Round-2: opus 0.10 / codex 0.07 -> consensus ~0.085 < market 0.115 -> NO-side edge ~+2.5pt < gate. NO-TRADE. Interest-conflict declared (VALUES.md #9): Claude family came in LOWER than Codex -- no pro-Anthropic inflation. We already hold KXLLM1-A (Anthropic) YES.

skipped.reason: [CYCLE 2] Settlement-clause ambiguity (memory-flagged MAXMON trap). Friendly title 'How high will BTC get' implies touch/max (market's 0.71), but rules_secondary literally describes a cumulative TRIMMED MEAN (top/bottom 20% trimmed) -> ~0.20 reading. ~50pt gap turns entirely on reading an ambiguous clause; being wrong on real money is catastrophic. Verified: BTC never touched $65k in July (month high $64,659 on 07-05; spot $63,278 on 07-09). Even under the favorable touch reading, +2.7% over 22 days makes market's 0.71 roughly fair -> no edge. PROTOCOL iron rule #4: unreadable settlement -> skip, do not force-estimate.
